verdict.summary: Guide is broadly on the right topic, but contains several material inaccuracies: especially the claimed 10-year renewal for national permanent residence, future/unverifiable nationality-law claims, an overstated EULTR interview rule, and incorrect/weak official references.

issues.problem: Portuguese permanent residence authorization has no time limit, but the residence title/card is not renewed every 10 years under the general rule. Lei 23/2007 art. 76 says the permanent residence title must be renewed every 5 years. The guide repeats the 10-year claim in several places.
issues.suggestion: Write: «Autorização de residência permanente не имеет срока действия, но título/cartão de residência, как правило, подлежит обновлению каждые 5 лет.»

issues.current: Autorização de Residência Permanente ... — продление каждые 10 лет.
issues.problem: Same incorrect 10-year renewal statement for national permanent residence. This is a core factual point and will mislead readers about renewal timing.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: «национальная autorização de residência permanente не ограничена по сроку, но сам título/cartão обновляется каждые 5 лет.»

issues.current: Карта ПМЖ приходит по почте. Действует 10 лет (национальная) или 5 лет (EULTR).
issues.problem: National permanent residence card validity is stated as 10 years, which conflicts with Lei 23/2007 art. 76. EULTR 5 years is fine.
issues.suggestion: Write: «Карта/титул: национальная permanent residence — обновление обычно каждые 5 лет; EULTR — 5 лет.»

issues.current: Нет обязательного «пересчёта» времени — продление каждые 10 лет
issues.problem: Again incorrectly says renewal every 10 years. The authorization is permanent, but the title/card renewal cycle is 5 years under the law.
issues.suggestion: Write: «Autorização permanente не требует нового 5-летнего “стажа”, но título/cartão обновляется каждые 5 лет.»

issues.current: По действующему праву натурализация после 5 лет легального проживания — 03.05.2026 Президент Сегуру промульгировал Decreto AR 48/XVII, который после публикации в DRE поднимет срок до 7 лет для CPLP/EU и 10 лет для остальных.
issues.problem: As of April 2026 this is future/unverifiable and contradicts the known status: the new nationality law was approved on 01.04.2026 and was awaiting promulgation. The guide should not state promulgation on 03.05.2026 or treat the change as legally certain before DRE publication.
issues.suggestion: Write as of April 2026: «По действующему праву — 5 лет легального проживания. 01.04.2026 Assembleia da República одобрила изменения, предлагающие увеличить сроки, но на момент проверки закон ожидал промульгации/публикации и ещё не вступил в силу; проверяйте DRE/IRN перед подачей.»
issues.source: Lei da Nacionalidade; Decreto da Assembleia da República approved 01.04.2026 awaiting promulgation as of April 2026

issues.current: Гражданство: отдельная процедура через IRN. Permanent residence НЕ обязателен. По действующему праву — 5 лет легального проживания; 03.05.2026 Президент Сегуру промульгировал Decreto AR 48/XVII — после публикации в DRE будет 7 лет для CPLP/EU и 10 лет для остальных
issues.problem: Same future/unverifiable nationality-law claim. As of April 2026 it should be described as an approved but not yet promulgated/published reform, not as already promulgated.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: «Гражданство: отдельная процедура через IRN; permanent residence не обязателен. На апрель 2026 действующее правило — 5 лет легального проживания. Реформа, одобренная AR 01.04.2026, ещё ожидала промульгации/публикации; применять новые сроки можно только после вступления закона в силу.»

issues.current: Обычно не требуется для национального ПМЖ, иногда вызывают. Для EU Long-Term — интервью обязательно.
issues.problem: The statement that an interview is mandatory for EU Long-Term Resident status is too categorical. The legal requirements are documentary/legal conditions; AIMA may call the applicant or require biometrics/attendance, but there is no general rule that a substantive interview is always mandatory.
issues.suggestion: Write: «Для EULTR AIMA может потребовать личную явку, биометрию и/или дополнительные вопросы/документы; не формулируйте это как всегда обязательное интервью, если AIMA прямо не указывает это для конкретного канала подачи.»

issues.current: Registo criminal: Португалия (бесплатно на eportugal.gov.pt) + страны проживания ≥ 1 года за последние 5 лет
issues.problem: Portuguese criminal record certificate is not generally free on ePortugal; the online certificate normally has a fee. Also for immigration procedures AIMA often requests authorization to consult the Portuguese criminal record instead of the applicant separately obtaining it.
issues.suggestion: Write: «Registo criminal português: обычно AIMA запрашивает autorização para consulta; если нужен certificado do registo criminal, он оформляется через ePortugal/serviços competentes и обычно оплачивается. Иностранные справки — по требованиям AIMA для стран проживания/происхождения, с апостилем/легализацией и переводом при необходимости.»

issues.current: Голосование на местных выборах (муниципальные) — для граждан ЕС и некоторых CPLP
issues.problem: This is incomplete/misleading. Local voting rights in Portugal are based on EU citizenship and reciprocity lists for certain third-country nationals, not simply «some CPLP». Some CPLP nationals qualify, but there are also non-CPLP countries, and not every resident foreigner qualifies.
issues.suggestion: Write: «Муниципальное голосование — только для граждан ЕС и граждан стран с признанной взаимностью/специальным режимом, после регистрации в recenseamento eleitoral; проверяйте актуальный список CNE/MAI. Для граждан РФ такого общего права нет.»
issues.source: Comissão Nacional de Eleições; Lei Eleitoral dos Órgãos das Autarquias Locais

issues.current: Подтверждение дохода: IRS Anexo A за 3 года, 12 месячных выписок по зарплате или Anexo B (для ИП)
issues.problem: This is overly prescriptive and partly technically confusing. Anexo A/B are annexes to the annual Modelo 3 IRS return, not standalone proof for “3 years” in the way written. AIMA document practice can vary; requiring exactly 3 years of IRS/anexos is not a statutory rule for all applicants.
issues.suggestion: Write more flexibly: «Подтверждение средств: последние IRS/nota de liquidação, recibos de vencimento/declaração da entidade patronal, recibos verdes/declaração de atividade, pensão, extractos bancários и др. — по актуальному checklist AIMA.»

issues.problem: This source title is misleading as a main citation for EU Long-Term Resident status. The operative regime is in Lei 23/2007 arts. 125-130 and Directive 2003/109/EC; later amendments may be relevant, but DL 29/2012 should not be presented as the principal EULTR source without explaining what it amended.
issues.suggestion: Use the consolidated Lei 23/2007 and Directive 2003/109/EC as primary sources. If citing later amendments, name exactly what each amendment changed.

issues.problem: The URL is only the AIMA homepage, not a specific procedure page or fee table. For a guide giving fees and document requirements, this is too weak and may become useless to readers.
issues.suggestion: Replace/add direct links to the current AIMA procedure page for «Autorização de Residência Permanente», «Estatuto de Residente de Longa Duração» and the AIMA fee table in force from 01.03.2026.
issues.source: AIMA official website; AIMA fee table effective 01.03.2026

issues.current: Costs list includes AIMA permanent (art. 76) but no separate EULTR fee.
issues.problem: The guide covers two different statuses, but the cost table only clearly prices national permanent residence. Readers may assume EULTR costs the same, which may not be true under the current AIMA fee table.
issues.suggestion: Add a separate line for «Estatuto de Residente de Longa Duração UE» using the AIMA fee table in force from 01.03.2026, or explicitly say the guide only prices national permanent residence.

issues.problem: The requested accuracy date is April 2026, but the guide claims verification after that date and includes May 2026 events. This makes the guide internally unsuitable for an April 2026 snapshot.
issues.suggestion: If the guide is meant to be valid as of April 2026, set lastVerified to an April 2026 date and remove future May 2026 claims. If it is a May 2026 guide, the review basis and all future events must be supported by DRE/official sources.

missing_topics: Direct link or citation to the AIMA fee table effective from 01.03.2026, including separate fees for national permanent residence and EULTR.
Clear explanation that national permanent residence authorization is indefinite but the physical residence title/card has its own renewal cycle.
For nationality, an April 2026 caveat that the reform approved on 01.04.2026 was awaiting promulgation/publication and was not yet in force.
Specific current AIMA checklists/pages for Autorização de Residência Permanente and Estatuto de Residente de Longa Duração instead of only the AIMA homepage.
Clarification of local-election voting rights by nationality/reciprocity list, not just EU and some CPLP.
